meta.title: War Profiteers Stock Tracker
meta.description: Defense contractor stock performance since the Iran War began on February 28, 2026
meta.methodology: Pre-war prices from market close Feb 27, 2026. Current prices estimated based on historical defense stock behavior during military conflicts. Market cap gains calculated from shares outstanding × price change.

companies.name: Lockheed Martin
companies.iranWarContracts: Javelin & PAC-3 production surge. $4.2B emergency supplemental requested.

companies.name: RTX Corporation
companies.iranWarContracts: Tomahawk & Patriot resupply. Raytheon missiles used in opening strikes.

companies.name: Northrop Grumman
companies.iranWarContracts: Global Hawk & Triton ISR flights over Iran. B-21 readiness surge.

companies.name: General Dynamics
companies.iranWarContracts: 155mm artillery shell production ramped. Submarine deployment support.

companies.name: Boeing
companies.iranWarContracts: F-15EX & F/A-18 sortie support. P-8 maritime patrol over Strait of Hormuz.

companies.name: L3Harris Technologies
companies.iranWarContracts: Electronic warfare systems deployed. Secure comms for theater operations.

companies.name: BAE Systems

companies.name: Leidos

companies.name: Huntington Ingalls
companies.iranWarContracts: Carrier strike group maintenance surge. Submarine readiness.

companies.name: CACI International

companies.name: Textron

companies.name: BWX Technologies

companies.name: Mercury Systems
companies.iranWarContracts: Sensor and radar subsystem demand surge for guided munitions.

topInstitutional.name: Vanguard Group
topInstitutional.note: Largest index fund provider. If you have a 401(k), you likely own defense stocks.

topInstitutional.name: BlackRock
topInstitutional.note: World's largest asset manager. Defense stocks in virtually every retirement fund.

topInstitutional.name: State Street
topInstitutional.note: Manages $4.1T in assets including massive defense positions.

topInstitutional.name: Capital Group
topInstitutional.note: Active fund manager with large defense overweight positions.

topInstitutional.name: Fidelity
topInstitutional.note: Major 401(k) provider. Millions of Americans unknowingly profit from war.

pensionFunds.name: CalPERS
pensionFunds.note: California public employee pensions tied to defense profits.

pensionFunds.name: CalSTRS
pensionFunds.note: California teachers' retirement fund profits from war.

pensionFunds.name: NY State Common
pensionFunds.note: New York state employees' pensions in defense stocks.

pensionFunds.name: Federal TSP
pensionFunds.note: Federal employees (including military) own defense stocks through TSP.

congressMembers.name: Multiple members
congressMembers.holdings: 67 Congress members hold defense stocks while voting on defense budgets
congressMembers.note: No law prohibits members of Congress from trading defense stocks while voting on defense spending.

followTheMoney.revolvingDoor: Over 2,000 former DoD officials now work for defense contractors
